input.content:
---
created: 2026-03-16T22:00
updated: 2026-03-16T22:00
type: report
tags:
  - moc
  - vault/health
  - y/2026
  - y/2026-03
aliases:
  - Vault Health Report
cssclasses:
  - wide
---

# Vault Health Report — March 2026

> [!abstract] TL;DR
> 17-year vault with **excellent daily capture discipline** and a solid clippings pipeline, but **PARA is operationally dead**, the **task backlog is unsustainable** (11,911 open / 8.6% completion), and the **knowledge graph is flat** (near-zero cross-linking). Overall grade: **B-**.

---

## 1. Vault Health Scorecard

| Dimension | Grade | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Daily Notes | **A+** | 8,723 files spanning 2009–2026. v2.0 template 100% compliant for 2025–2026. |
| Clippings | **A** | 438 files, 99.8% frontmatter compliance, proper source metadata. |
| BMAD Framework | **B+** | 25 agents configured across 6 modules; 5 workflows operational. |
| Git / LFS | **B+** | Clean status, 79 LFS-tracked files, auto-backup active. |
| Inbox Processing | **A+** | Zero unprocessed files — inbox is empty. |
| Books Collection | **C+** | 791 files, but inconsistent metadata across the collection. |
| PARA Structure | **D** | `01 - Projects` has 1 real file. `02 - Areas` and `03 - Resources` are placeholders only. |
| Task Management | **D** | 11,911 incomplete tasks, 1,029 complete — **8.6% completion rate**. No review mechanism. |
| Knowledge Graph | **D** | 0.04–0.08 wikilinks per line. Minimal cross-linking; vault is document-centric. |
| Tag System | **D+** | Only temporal tags (`y/YYYY`, `y/YYYY-MM`) actively used. Topic tags absent. |
| MOCs / Navigation | **D** | 1 `Projects.base` file. No other Maps of Content. |
| Storage Hygiene | **C** | ~900 MB orphaned worktree. 11,701 archive files. |

> [!tip] Overall Grade: **B-**
> The vault excels at *capture* but underperforms at *retrieval, review, and connection*.

---

## 2. What's Working Well

> [!success] Preserve these — they are load-bearing habits

1. **Daily note discipline** — 17 years of continuous journaling is genuinely rare. The v2.0 template migration (507 files, Feb 2025) shows active maintenance.
2. **Clippings pipeline** — 99.8% frontmatter compliance with consistent source metadata. This is the model to replicate elsewhere.
3. **Inbox zero** — The capture-to-inbox flow works; nothing piles up.
4. **BMAD configuration** — 25 agents across 6 modules with 5 operational workflows. Infrastructure is ready even if underused.
5. **Auto-backup** — Git + LFS with automatic commits. Data loss risk is minimal.

---

## 3. Critical Findings

### 3.1 PARA Is Operationally Dead

`01 - Projects` contains a single real file. `02 - Areas` and `03 - Resources` hold only placeholders. The PARA folders exist structurally but serve no functional purpose — all actual work lives in daily notes as inline tasks and log entries.

**Root cause:** Daily capture is frictionless; moving items into PARA folders requires a second step that never became habit. There's no periodic review to surface items from daily notes into project/area notes.

### 3.2 Task Backlog Is Unsustainable

11,911 incomplete tasks vs 1,029 completed — an **8.6% completion rate**. With task management staying vault-only, this is the single highest-impact problem.

**Root cause:** Tasks are captured in daily notes but never reviewed, triaged, or closed. There's no weekly review, no task dashboard, and no mechanism to distinguish "still relevant" from "silently abandoned."

### 3.3 Knowledge Graph Is Flat

Wikilink density is 0.04–0.08 links per line — almost entirely navigation links (`<< prev | today | next >>`). Notes don't reference each other meaningfully. The vault functions as 11,000+ independent documents, not a connected knowledge base.

**Root cause:** Daily notes are designed for capture, not connection. Without MOCs or topic notes to link *through*, there's no natural place to create cross-references.

### 3.4 Tags Are Underutilized

Only temporal tags (`y/2026`, `y/2026-03`) are actively used. No topic, project, or status tags exist despite 37 plugins installed. The tag system adds no findability beyond what a date filter provides.

**Root cause:** No tag taxonomy was defined upfront, and retroactively tagging 11,000+ notes is impractical. Tags need to be introduced going forward, not backfilled.

### 3.5 Books Collection Is Inconsistent

791 book note files with mixed metadata schemas — some have author/rating/genre, others have minimal or no frontmatter. No standard template enforced.

**Root cause:** Notes accumulated over years under different conventions. No single template was established early enough to prevent drift.

---

## 4. Prioritized Recommendations

> [!warning] Scope
> These are recommendations only. No changes were made to the vault. Task management and PARA activation are prioritized per Juliano's direction.

### Tier 1: Quick Wins (< 30 min each)

| # | Action | Impact |
|---|---|---|
| Q1 | **Clean orphaned worktree** — `rm -rf .worktrees/fix-copy-and-paste/` recovers ~900 MB | Storage |
| Q2 | **Update memory bank** — `memory-bank/activeContext.md` is frozen at Nov 2025; refresh to current state | Context |
| Q3 | **Fix project file frontmatter** — Add `type: project` and `status: active` to the one real project file | PARA |
| Q4 | **Create 3–5 Area notes** — `02 - Areas/Cloud Ops.md`, `Security.md`, `DevOps.md`, `AI-ML.md`, `Personal.md` with proper frontmatter | PARA |
| Q5 | **Task shape analysis** — Count incomplete tasks per month to see where the backlog concentrates (likely pre-2025) | Tasks |

### Tier 2: High Priority (1–2 hrs each)

> [!important] These target the two biggest gaps: task management and PARA activation.

**M1: Weekly Review Template**
Create a periodic note template (weekly) that surfaces:
- Incomplete tasks from the past 7 days
- New clippings to process or promote
- Active projects needing attention
- A "close/defer/delete" triage step for stale tasks

Use the periodic-notes plugin with a `06 - Daily/YYYY/WW` structure or a dedicated `99 - Meta/Reviews/` folder.

**M2: Task Rollup Dashboard (.base file)**
Create a `.base` file at `00 - Maps of Content/Tasks.base` that:
- Filters all vault files containing incomplete tasks
- Groups by folder or `::` project prefix patterns found in daily notes
- Shows task count, oldest task date, and last-modified date
- Provides a single view of the full backlog

**M3: PARA Activation**
Activate PARA by connecting daily note patterns to dedicated project notes:

1. **Identify active projects** from recent daily notes — look for recurring task prefixes like `hypera`, `cafehyna`, `antigravity`, `infracost`
2. **Create project notes** in `01 - Projects/` with proper frontmatter (`type: project`, `status: active`, `priority: high|medium|low`)
3. **Create area notes** in `02 - Areas/` for ongoing responsibilities (Cloud Ops, Security, DevOps, AI/ML, Personal Finance)
4. **Link daily tasks to projects** using `[[Project Name]]` wikilinks in task lines going forward
5. **The `Projects.base` MOC already exists** — once project notes have proper frontmatter, the dashboard populates automatically

> [!tip] Start small
> Create 3 project notes and 3 area notes. Don't try to retroactively categorize everything. Let PARA grow from daily use.

**M4: Topic MOCs**
Create `.base` files for the vault's dominant knowledge domains:
- `00 - Maps of Content/Kubernetes.base` — filter by tag or folder
- `00 - Maps of Content/Azure.base`
- `00 - Maps of Content/DevOps.base`
- `00 - Maps of Content/Security.base`
- `00 - Maps of Content/AI-ML.base`

These give the knowledge graph structure without requiring manual linking of every note.

**M5: Tag Taxonomy Seed**
Introduce 5–10 topic tags for use in daily notes **going forward**:
- `#topic/kubernetes`, `#topic/azure`, `#topic/security`, `#topic/devops`, `#topic/ai`
- `#project/hypera`, `#project/cafehyna`, `#project/antigravity`
- `#type/decision`, `#type/learning`, `#type/blocker`

Don't backfill. Add these when touching notes naturally.

### Tier 3: Structural (half-day+)

**S1: Task Bankruptcy**
Declare bankruptcy on pre-2026 incomplete tasks:
1. Run a count of incomplete tasks by year
2. Archive everything pre-2026 as "historical" — mark with `- [~]` or move to a canceled state
3. Start 2026 with a clean task baseline
4. Use the weekly review (M1) to prevent re-accumulation

**S2: Book Note Template**
Create a standard template for `08 - books/` with fields: `title`, `author`, `rating`, `genre`, `status` (reading/read/abandoned), `date_started`, `date_finished`. Apply only to new entries — don't backfill the 791 existing files.

**S3: Progressive Linking**
Adopt a rule: when touching any note for any reason, add 1–2 wikilinks to related notes. This builds the knowledge graph organically without a dedicated "linking sprint."

**S4: Clippings-to-Resources Pipeline**
Periodically promote high-value clippings from `Clippings/` to `03 - Resources/` with added context. The clippings pipeline is the vault's strongest system — extend it into the knowledge layer.

---

## 5. Anti-Patterns to Avoid

> [!danger] These will waste time or break what works

1. **Don't bulk-migrate pre-2025 notes** — They're historical records. Changing their structure risks breaking links and provides minimal return on 8,000+ files.
2. **Don't force zettelkasten on daily notes** — The daily capture workflow works. Atomic notes are a different paradigm; mixing them creates friction without benefit.
3. **Don't create an elaborate tag taxonomy upfront** — Start with 5–10 tags. Expand only when you hit a real findability gap. Premature taxonomy is maintenance debt.
4. **Don't try to complete the 11,911 task backlog** — Most are silently abandoned. Triage and declare bankruptcy on anything older than 90 days.
5. **Don't use BMAD for vault housekeeping** — BMAD is configured for software development workflows. Vault maintenance is simpler than what BMAD orchestrates; use native Obsidian tools (Bases, Dataview, templates).

---

## 6. Vault Statistics Summary

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Total markdown files | 11,334 |
| Total attachments | 24,183 |
| Daily notes | 8,723 |
| Book notes | 791 |
| Clippings | 438 |
| Installed plugins | 37 |
| BMAD agents | 25 |
| LFS-tracked files | 79 |
| Incomplete tasks | 11,911 |
| Completed tasks | 1,029 |
| Task completion rate | 8.6% |
| Archive files | 11,701 |
| Orphaned worktree size | ~900 MB |

---

## 7. Next Steps

If you want to start implementing, the recommended order is:

1. **Q1–Q5** (quick wins) — clean worktree, update memory bank, seed PARA
2. **M2** (task dashboard) — get visibility into the backlog first
3. **M1** (weekly review) — establish the review habit
4. **S1** (task bankruptcy) — clear the backlog after you can see it
5. **M3** (PARA activation) — create project/area notes from daily patterns
6. **M4–M5** (MOCs + tags) — add findability layers

---

*Generated 2026-03-16 by PAI vault analysis. Data sourced from direct vault exploration across three parallel agents.*

input.description: Check if Tasks plugin is installed

input.content:
filters:
  and:
    - file.inFolder("06 - Daily/2026")
    - 'type == "daily-note"'

formulas:
  word_estimate: '(file.size / 5).round(0)'
  last_modified: 'file.mtime.format("YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm")'
  age_days: '(today() - date(date_formatted)).days.round(0)'
  month: 'if(date_formatted, date_formatted.slice(0, 7), "")'

properties:
  formula.word_estimate:
    displayName: "~Words"
  formula.last_modified:
    displayName: "Modified"
  formula.age_days:
    displayName: "Days Ago"
  formula.month:
    displayName: "Month"

views:
  - type: table
    name: "Recent Activity (Last 30 Days)"
    filters:
      and:
        - 'if(date_formatted, (today() - date(date_formatted)).days <= 30, false)'
    order:
      - file.name
      - formula.age_days
      - formula.word_estimate
      - formula.last_modified
    summaries:
      formula.word_estimate: Average
      formula.age_days: Min

  - type: table
    name: "March 2026"
    filters:
      and:
        - file.hasTag("y/2026-03")
    order:
      - file.name
      - formula.word_estimate
      - formula.last_modified
    summaries:
      formula.word_estimate: Sum

  - type: table
    name: "February 2026"
    filters:
      and:
        - file.hasTag("y/2026-02")
    order:
      - file.name
      - formula.word_estimate
      - formula.last_modified
    summaries:
      formula.word_estimate: Sum

  - type: table
    name: "January 2026"
    filters:
      and:
        - file.hasTag("y/2026-01")
    order:
      - file.name
      - formula.word_estimate
      - formula.last_modified
    summaries:
      formula.word_estimate: Sum

  - type: table
    name: "All 2026"
    order:
      - file.name
      - formula.month
      - formula.word_estimate
      - formula.last_modified
    groupBy:
      property: formula.month
      direction: DESC
    summaries:
      formula.word_estimate: Sum

---
created: 2026-03-17T00:00
updated: 2026-03-17T00:00
type: moc
tags:
  - moc
  - tasks
  - y/2026
aliases:
  - Task Dashboard
cssclasses:
  - wide
---

# Tasks Dashboard

> [!abstract] Overview
> Vault-wide task visibility using Dataview TASK queries. Tasks live as inline `- [ ]` items in daily notes, using `::` separators for project/context hierarchy (e.g., `hypera :: cafehyna :: argocd`).

---

## This Week (Last 7 Days)

```dataview
TASK
FROM "06 - Daily/2026"
WHERE !completed
WHERE file.cday >= date(today) - dur(7 days)
SORT file.cday DESC
```

---

## Recent Open Tasks (Last 30 Days)

```dataview
TASK
FROM "06 - Daily/2026"
WHERE !completed
WHERE file.cday >= date(today) - dur(30 days)
SORT file.cday DESC
```

---

## By Project: Hypera

> [!note] Work tasks — largest category

```dataview
TASK
FROM "06 - Daily/2026"
WHERE !completed AND contains(text, "hypera")
SORT file.cday DESC
```

### Cafehyna (Sub-project)

```dataview
TASK
FROM "06 - Daily/2026"
WHERE !completed AND contains(text, "cafehyna")
SORT file.cday DESC
```

---

## By Project: Claude Code / PAI

```dataview
TASK
FROM "06 - Daily/2026"
WHERE !completed AND contains(text, "cc ::")
SORT file.cday DESC
```

---

## By Project: BMAD

```dataview
TASK
FROM "06 - Daily/2026"
WHERE !completed AND contains(text, "bmad")
SORT file.cday DESC
```

---

## By Project: Azure / Microsoft

```dataview
TASK
FROM "06 - Daily/2026"
WHERE !completed AND (contains(text, "azure") OR contains(text, "microsoft"))
SORT file.cday DESC
```

---

## Personal / Other

> [!tip] Tasks not matching any project prefix above

```dataview
TASK
FROM "06 - Daily/2026"
WHERE !completed AND !contains(text, "hypera") AND !contains(text, "cc ::") AND !contains(text, "bmad") AND !contains(text, "azure") AND !contains(text, "microsoft")
SORT file.cday DESC
```

---

## Completed Tasks (2026)

```dataview
TASK
FROM "06 - Daily/2026"
WHERE completed
SORT file.cday DESC
```

---

## Backlog Shape

> [!warning] Task distribution by month — helps identify where bankruptcy makes sense

```dataview
TABLE length(filter(file.tasks, (t) => !t.completed)) AS "Open Tasks", length(filter(file.tasks, (t) => t.completed)) AS "Done"
FROM "06 - Daily"
WHERE length(file.tasks) > 0
SORT file.name DESC
LIMIT 50
```

---

## Daily Activity

> [!info] Note-level view of 2026 daily notes — see [[Daily Activity.base]] for the full interactive view

![[Daily Activity.base#Recent Activity (Last 30 Days)]]

---

*See also: [[Vault Health Report 2026-03]] for the full vault analysis and improvement recommendations.*

Count all incomplete tasks (`- [ ]`) and completed tasks (`- [x]`) in the Obsidian vault at `/Users/juliano.barbosa/.obsidian/vaults/.obsidian-barbosa-0/06 - Daily/` broken down by year. 

Use Grep with output_mode "count" to search each year folder. The years are: 2009 through 2026 (check which year folders exist first with Glob).

Return a markdown table with columns: Year, Incomplete, Completed, Total, Completion Rate %.

This is research only - do NOT write any files.

input.prompt:
Analyze recent daily notes in `/Users/juliano.barbosa/.obsidian/vaults/.obsidian-barbosa-0/06 - Daily/2026/` to identify the most common project prefixes used in task lines (`- [ ]` items).

Tasks use `::` as separators, e.g., `hypera :: cafehyna :: argocd`.

Search for all incomplete tasks across 2026 daily notes using Grep, then extract and count the top-level project prefixes (the first word/phrase before the first `::`).

Return:
1. A ranked list of project prefixes with counts
2. For each top prefix, the most common sub-prefixes (second level after `::`)
3. Which prefixes appear in the last 30 days vs older

This is research only - do NOT write any files.

input.content:
---
created: 2026-03-17T00:00
updated: 2026-03-17T00:00
type: area
tags:
  - area/cloud-ops
  - azure
aliases:
  - Cloud Ops
---

# Cloud Operations

> [!abstract] Ongoing responsibility
> Azure infrastructure management, cost optimization (FinOps), networking, and platform operations at Hypera.

## Scope

- Azure subscription and resource management
- Networking (VNets, NSGs, subnets, CIDR planning)
- Cost management and FinOps (reservations, billing, waste discovery)
- Microsoft quota and support requests
- Policy and compliance

## Related Projects

- [[rg-hypera-packer-image-vnet-task]]

## Key Resources

- Azure Portal
- Infracost dashboards
- `infra-redes` repository (CIDR documentation)

---
created: 2026-03-17T00:00
updated: 2026-03-17T00:00
type: area
tags:
  - area/devops
  - kubernetes
  - argocd
aliases:
  - DevOps
  - Platform Engineering
---

# DevOps Platform

> [!abstract] Ongoing responsibility
> Kubernetes platform, CI/CD pipelines, GitOps (ArgoCD), and developer experience at Hypera/Cafehyna.

## Scope

- Kubernetes cluster management (AKS)
- ArgoCD and GitOps workflows (ApplicationSets, Image Updater)
- Azure DevOps pipelines and self-hosted agents (Blue Agent)
- Helm, Kustomize, and Terraform patterns
- Container image management and registry

## Key Repositories

- `cafehyna` — main platform repository
- `rg-hypera-aks-python-infra` — AKS Python infrastructure

---
created: 2026-03-17T00:00
updated: 2026-03-17T00:00
type: area
tags:
  - area/security
aliases:
  - Security
  - InfoSec
---

# Security

> [!abstract] Ongoing responsibility
> Information security practices, vulnerability management, and compliance across cloud infrastructure.

## Scope

- Azure security posture (Defender, Sentinel)
- RBAC and identity management (PIM, Entra ID)
- Secret management (Key Vault)
- Security assessments and penetration testing
- Compliance and audit requirements
- Copilot security configuration

---
created: 2026-03-17T00:00
updated: 2026-03-17T00:00
type: area
tags:
  - area/ai
  - claude-code
  - bmad
aliases:
  - AI
  - Automation
---

# AI & Automation

> [!abstract] Ongoing responsibility
> Personal AI infrastructure (PAI), Claude Code workflows, BMAD framework, and automation tooling.

## Scope

- PAI (Personal AI Infrastructure) — skills, algorithms, agents
- Claude Code configuration and MCP servers
- BMAD framework (agents, workflows, modules)
- Obsidian automation (Templater, Dataview, Bases)
- AI-assisted development workflows

## Key Tools

- Claude Code (PAI)
- BMAD Core Platform (`_bmad/`)
- Obsidian plugins (Templater, Dataview, Tasks)

---
created: 2026-03-17T00:00
updated: 2026-03-17T00:00
type: area
tags:
  - area/personal
aliases:
  - Personal
---

# Personal Development

> [!abstract] Ongoing responsibility
> Learning, health, finances, and personal growth.

## Scope

- MBA Internacional program
- Technical learning and certifications
- Health and medical appointments
- Personal finance
- Reading and book notes (`08 - books/`)

---
created: <% tp.file.creation_date("YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm") %>
updated: <% tp.file.creation_date("YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm") %>
type: weekly-review
tags:
  - review/weekly
  - y/<% tp.date.now("YYYY") %>
  - y/<% tp.date.now("YYYY-MM") %>
aliases:
  - Week <% tp.date.now("WW") %> Review
cssclasses:
  - wide
week_start: <% tp.date.now("YYYY-MM-DD", 0, tp.file.title, "YYYY-[W]WW") %>
week_end: <% tp.date.now("YYYY-MM-DD", 6, tp.file.title, "YYYY-[W]WW") %>
---

# Weekly Review — <% tp.file.title %>

## 1. Incomplete Tasks (This Week)

> [!warning] Triage each: **close**, **defer**, or **keep**

```dataview
TASK
FROM "06 - Daily/<% tp.date.now("YYYY") %>"
WHERE !completed
WHERE file.cday >= date("<% tp.date.now("YYYY-MM-DD", -6) %>") AND file.cday <= date("<% tp.date.now("YYYY-MM-DD") %>")
SORT file.cday DESC
```

## 2. Completed Tasks

```dataview
TASK
FROM "06 - Daily/<% tp.date.now("YYYY") %>"
WHERE completed
WHERE file.cday >= date("<% tp.date.now("YYYY-MM-DD", -6) %>") AND file.cday <= date("<% tp.date.now("YYYY-MM-DD") %>")
SORT file.cday DESC
```

## 3. New Clippings

```dataview
LIST
FROM "Clippings"
WHERE file.cday >= date("<% tp.date.now("YYYY-MM-DD", -6) %>") AND file.cday <= date("<% tp.date.now("YYYY-MM-DD") %>")
SORT file.ctime DESC
```

## 4. Active Projects Check

```dataview
TABLE status, priority, updated
FROM "01 - Projects"
WHERE type = "project" AND status = "active"
SORT priority ASC
```

> [!todo] For each active project:
> - Is it still active? Update status if not.
> - What's the next action?
> - Any blockers?

## 5. Wins This Week

-

## 6. Lessons Learned

-

## 7. Next Week Focus

> [!important] Top 3 priorities for next week

1.
2.
3.

## 8. Stale Task Cleanup

> [!danger] Tasks older than 30 days — consider closing or deferring

```dataview
TASK
FROM "06 - Daily"
WHERE !completed
WHERE file.cday < date("<% tp.date.now("YYYY-MM-DD", -30) %>") AND file.cday >= date("<% tp.date.now("YYYY-MM-DD", -60) %>")
SORT file.cday ASC
LIMIT 20
```

filters:
  or:
    - file.hasTag("kubernetes")
    - file.hasTag("k8s")
    - file.hasTag("area/devops")
    - file.hasTag("argocd")

formulas:
  last_modified: 'file.mtime.format("YYYY-MM-DD")'
  age_days: '(today() - file.ctime).days.round(0)'
  source: 'if(file.folder.contains("Clippings"), "Clipping", if(file.folder.contains("Daily"), "Daily", if(file.folder.contains("Projects"), "Project", file.folder)))'

properties:
  formula.last_modified:
    displayName: "Modified"
  formula.age_days:
    displayName: "Age"
  formula.source:
    displayName: "Source"

views:
  - type: table
    name: "All Kubernetes Notes"
    order:
      - file.name
      - formula.source
      - file.tags
      - formula.last_modified
    summaries:
      formula.age_days: Average

  - type: table
    name: "Recent (Last 90 Days)"
    filters:
      and:
        - 'file.mtime >= today() + "-90d"'
    order:
      - file.name
      - formula.source
      - formula.last_modified

filters:
  or:
    - file.hasTag("azure")
    - file.hasTag("area/cloud-ops")
    - file.hasTag("azure/networking")

formulas:
  last_modified: 'file.mtime.format("YYYY-MM-DD")'
  source: 'if(file.folder.contains("Clippings"), "Clipping", if(file.folder.contains("Daily"), "Daily", if(file.folder.contains("Projects"), "Project", if(file.folder.contains("Areas"), "Area", file.folder))))'

properties:
  formula.last_modified:
    displayName: "Modified"
  formula.source:
    displayName: "Source"

views:
  - type: table
    name: "All Azure Notes"
    order:
      - file.name
      - formula.source
      - file.tags
      - formula.last_modified

  - type: table
    name: "Recent (Last 90 Days)"
    filters:
      and:
        - 'file.mtime >= today() + "-90d"'
    order:
      - file.name
      - formula.source
      - formula.last_modified

    - file.hasTag("security")
    - file.hasTag("area/security")

# Cafehyna Platform

> [!abstract] Active project
> Hypera's internal DevOps/GitOps platform. Kubernetes (AKS), ArgoCD ApplicationSets, CI/CD pipelines, and infrastructure automation.

## Active Work Streams

- ArgoCD ApplicationSet deployments and auto-deploy
- ADP Agent (Azure DevOps Pipeline self-hosted agents)
- Network calculator and CIDR segregation
- Elasticsearch configuration
- MySQL 5.4 to 8 upgrade
- Kargo integration
- Parallel execution workflows

## Key Repositories

- `cafehyna` — main platform repo
- `rg-hypera-aks-python-infra` — AKS Python infrastructure

## Related Areas

- [[Cloud Operations]]

input.old_string:
> [!warning] Scope
> These are recommendations only. No changes were made to the vault. Task management and PARA activation are prioritized per Juliano's direction.

### Tier 1: Quick Wins (< 30 min each)

| # | Action | Impact |
|---|---|---|
| Q1 | **Clean orphaned worktree** — `rm -rf .worktrees/fix-copy-and-paste/` recovers ~900 MB | Storage |
| Q2 | **Update memory bank** — `memory-bank/activeContext.md` is frozen at Nov 2025; refresh to current state | Context |
| Q3 | **Fix project file frontmatter** — Add `type: project` and `status: active` to the one real project file | PARA |
| Q4 | **Create 3–5 Area notes** — `02 - Areas/Cloud Ops.md`, `Security.md`, `DevOps.md`, `AI-ML.md`, `Personal.md` with proper frontmatter | PARA |
| Q5 | **Task shape analysis** — Count incomplete tasks per month to see where the backlog concentrates (likely pre-2025) | Tasks |

### Tier 2: High Priority (1–2 hrs each)

> [!important] These target the two biggest gaps: task management and PARA activation.

**M1: Weekly Review Template**
Create a periodic note template (weekly) that surfaces:
- Incomplete tasks from the past 7 days
- New clippings to process or promote
- Active projects needing attention
- A "close/defer/delete" triage step for stale tasks

Use the periodic-notes plugin with a `06 - Daily/YYYY/WW` structure or a dedicated `99 - Meta/Reviews/` folder.

**M2: Task Rollup Dashboard** ==DONE==
Created [[Tasks Dashboard]] (Dataview TASK queries grouped by project) and [[Daily Activity.base]] (note-level activity view). Tasks are inline `- [ ]` items — Bases can't index them, so Dataview provides the task-level granularity while the `.base` file shows daily note activity.

**M3: PARA Activation**
Activate PARA by connecting daily note patterns to dedicated project notes:

1. **Identify active projects** from recent daily notes — look for recurring task prefixes like `hypera`, `cafehyna`, `antigravity`, `infracost`
2. **Create project notes** in `01 - Projects/` with proper frontmatter (`type: project`, `status: active`, `priority: high|medium|low`)
3. **Create area notes** in `02 - Areas/` for ongoing responsibilities (Cloud Ops, Security, DevOps, AI/ML, Personal Finance)
4. **Link daily tasks to projects** using `[[Project Name]]` wikilinks in task lines going forward
5. **The `Projects.base` MOC already exists** — once project notes have proper frontmatter, the dashboard populates automatically

> [!tip] Start small
> Create 3 project notes and 3 area notes. Don't try to retroactively categorize everything. Let PARA grow from daily use.

**M4: Topic MOCs**
Create `.base` files for the vault's dominant knowledge domains:
- `00 - Maps of Content/Kubernetes.base` — filter by tag or folder
- `00 - Maps of Content/Azure.base`
- `00 - Maps of Content/DevOps.base`
- `00 - Maps of Content/Security.base`
- `00 - Maps of Content/AI-ML.base`

These give the knowledge graph structure without requiring manual linking of every note.

**M5: Tag Taxonomy Seed**
Introduce 5–10 topic tags for use in daily notes **going forward**:
- `#topic/kubernetes`, `#topic/azure`, `#topic/security`, `#topic/devops`, `#topic/ai`
- `#project/hypera`, `#project/cafehyna`, `#project/antigravity`
- `#type/decision`, `#type/learning`, `#type/blocker`

Don't backfill. Add these when touching notes naturally.

### Tier 1: Quick Wins (< 30 min each)

| # | Action | Impact | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 | **Clean orphaned worktree** — `rm -rf .worktrees/fix-copy-and-paste/` recovers **3.6 GB** (larger than estimated) | Storage | Needs confirmation |
| Q2 | **Update memory bank** — `memory-bank/activeContext.md` is frozen at **March 2025** (contains template placeholders); refresh to current state | Context | Manual |
| Q3 | **Fix project file frontmatter** — Added `type: project`, `status: complete`, `priority: medium` to `rg-hypera-packer-image-vnet-task.md` | PARA | ==DONE== |
| Q4 | **Create 5 Area notes** — [[Cloud Operations]], [[Security]], [[DevOps Platform]], [[AI & Automation]], [[Personal Development]] | PARA | ==DONE== |
| Q5 | **Task shape analysis** — Analysis in progress (background agent counting tasks per year) | Tasks | In progress |

### Tier 2: High Priority (1–2 hrs each)

> [!important] These target the two biggest gaps: task management and PARA activation.

**M1: Weekly Review Template** ==DONE==
Created `99 - Meta/00 - Templates/daily/weekly-review.md` — Templater template with 8 sections:
1. Incomplete tasks (this week) with triage prompt
2. Completed tasks
3. New clippings
4. Active projects check
5. Wins / Lessons learned
6. Next week focus (top 3)
7. Stale task cleanup (30+ days old)

> [!tip] To use: Configure the periodic-notes plugin to use this template for weekly notes, or trigger manually via Templater.

**M2: Task Rollup Dashboard** ==DONE==
Created [[Tasks Dashboard]] (Dataview TASK queries grouped by project) and [[Daily Activity.base]] (note-level activity view). Tasks are inline `- [ ]` items — Bases can't index them, so Dataview provides the task-level granularity while the `.base` file shows daily note activity.

**M3: PARA Activation** ==DONE==
Activated PARA with real content:

**Projects created** (3 active + 1 existing):
- [[Cafehyna Platform]] — `priority: high`, DevOps/GitOps platform
- [[PAI Development]] — `priority: high`, Claude Code and AI tooling
- [[Hypera FinOps]] — `priority: medium`, Azure cost optimization
- [[rg-hypera-packer-image-vnet-task]] — `status: complete` (frontmatter fixed)

**Areas created** (5):
- [[Cloud Operations]] — Azure infra, networking, FinOps
- [[DevOps Platform]] — K8s, ArgoCD, CI/CD
- [[Security]] — InfoSec, RBAC, compliance
- [[AI & Automation]] — PAI, Claude Code, BMAD
- [[Personal Development]] — MBA, learning, health

The [[Projects.base]] MOC now populates automatically with the 3 active projects.

> [!todo] Going forward
> Link daily tasks to projects using `[[Project Name]]` wikilinks in task lines. The dashboards will surface them automatically.

**M4: Topic MOCs** ==DONE==
Created 4 `.base` files in `00 - Maps of Content/`:
- [[Kubernetes.base]] — filters `kubernetes`, `k8s`, `area/devops`, `argocd` tags
- [[Azure.base]] — filters `azure`, `area/cloud-ops` tags
- [[Security.base]] — filters `security`, `area/security` tags
- [[AI-ML.base]] — filters `area/ai`, `claude-code`, `bmad` tags

Each has two views: "All Notes" and "Recent (Last 90 Days)". These will populate as topic tags are added to notes.

> [!note] DevOps MOC
> Not created separately — covered by [[Kubernetes.base]] (which includes ArgoCD) and the [[DevOps Platform]] area note.

**M5: Tag Taxonomy Seed**
The following tags are now seeded via the Area and Project notes created above:
- `area/cloud-ops`, `area/devops`, `area/security`, `area/ai`, `area/personal`
- `project/cafehyna`, `project/hypera`, `project/pai`, `project/cc`, `project/finops`
- `azure`, `kubernetes`, `argocd`, `claude-code`, `bmad`

> [!todo] Going forward
> Add these tags when touching daily notes naturally. Don't backfill.

## 7. Next Steps

If you want to start implementing, the recommended order is:

1. **Q1–Q5** (quick wins) — clean worktree, update memory bank, seed PARA
2. **M2** (task dashboard) — get visibility into the backlog first
3. **M1** (weekly review) — establish the review habit
4. **S1** (task bankruptcy) — clear the backlog after you can see it
5. **M3** (PARA activation) — create project/area notes from daily patterns
6. **M4–M5** (MOCs + tags) — add findability layers
